what_it_does: This file is the prescriptive module constitution for the file-discovered generation-policy registry; it defines what a generation policy is, the required schema, the live regimes, and the rules for adding or consuming them.
observations: Generation policies are declared DATA, not static code constants.
Registry is directory-discovered: one file per regime under generation_policies/.
Required schema fields: id, rep_penalty_ladder, diff_against_source; optional json_schema/temperature/budget/desc.
Live set lists two regimes: capture_default and prose_default.
Malformed entries must fail loud at discovery; non-GENERATION_POLICY files are skipped.
Deliberately avoids a .resolve()-named method to stay floor-safe for COG_SOURCES.
Acceptance test tests/generation_policies_acceptance.py asserts the live set is reflected here.
Seam points to runtime/generation_policies.py:GenerationPolicyRegistry and a future run_role wiring pass.
standouts: Makes the 'NOTHING static' law concrete by turning sampling hyperparameters into registry rows.
Uses a repetition_penalty LADDER (default -> escalate-on-length -> fail-loud degenerate-loop) to address grammar-constrained repetition loops.
Includes a diff_against_source guard so enumeration is never silently penalized.
Two reference regimes prove the ladder can be multi-rung or single-rung and flags vary per content type.
conventions: Follows the file-discovered registry pattern mirroring roles/skills/projections/node-types.
Self-registering module files: drop a file to add a row, remove a file to unregister.
Constitution format with explicit relates-to/governs/read-next links.
Fail-loud validation for malformed registry entries.
concerns: The 'live set' list in this file must be manually kept in sync with directory files or acceptance tests fail.
Optional schema fields are not described with validation rules or consumption semantics here.
The run_role wiring and future create_generation_policy seam are flagged but not implemented in this file.
No mention of how rediscovery is triggered or how often the registry is rescanned.
notes: This constitution turns generation hyperparameters into a file-discovered, data-declared registry and forbids static constants.
questions: How is registry rediscovery triggered and on what schedule?
What exact validation logic enforces the schema and the id-must-equal-stem rule?
How does run_role select and apply a generation regime?
What is the C9.2 forbidden .resolve token referenced in the floor rule?
What does the acceptance test assert beyond list reflection?
How are optional fields like json_schema, budget, and temperature consumed by callers?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it both declares the registry constitution and documents the live policies, schema, guarantees, and extension rules.
intention_signals: It is trying to institutionalize the 'no static generation knobs' principle by making sampling regimes first-class, discoverable registry entries with data-driven escalation ladders and loss guards.
novelty: It treats sampling parameters (rep_penalty, temperature, json_schema) as registry rows with per-regime ladders and a diff-against-source flag, rather than inline code constants.
contribution: It contributes a concrete schema and registry pattern for generation policies, two reference regimes (capture_default, prose_default), and a prescriptive rule set that keeps generation parameters out of code.
summary_for_embedding: company/generation_policies/AGENTS.md is the prescriptive module constitution for a file-discovered generation-policy registry that declares per-content sampling regimes as data, including repetition-penalty ladders and diff-against-source guards.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make the 'no static generation knobs' rule concrete by registering generation regimes as discoverable files with fail-loud validation and pure-read consumption.
